what: Custom Formatted Console Messages with Timing Support
title: Custom Formatted Console Messages with Timing Support
text: Custom Formatted Console Messages Timing Support lightweight message system relying purely base R. Comes built-pre styled message types provides easy way create custom messages. Supports individually styled colored text well timing information. Designed make console output informative visually organized.

title: Custom Formatted Console Messages with Timing Support
code: # Official release install.packages("printify")  # Development version devtools::install_github("s3rdia/printify")  install.packages('printify', repos = c('https://s3rdia.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))  pak::pak("s3rdia/printify")

title: Custom Formatted Console Messages with Timing Support
code: library(printify)  # Example messages print_message("NOTE", c("Just a quick note that you can also insert e.g.[? a / ]variable",                         "name[?s] like this: [listing].",                         "Depending on the number of variables you can also alter the text."),              listing = c("age", "state", "NUTS3"))  print_message("WARNING", "Just a quick [#FF00FF colored warning]!")  print_message("ERROR", "Or a [b]bold[/b], [i]italic[/i] and [u]underlined[/u] error.")  print_message("NEUTRAL", c("You can also just output [u]plain text[/u] if you like and use",                            "[#FFFF00 [b]all the different[/b] [i]formatting options.[/i]]"))  # Different headlines print_headline("This is a headline")  print_headline("[#00FFFF This is a different headline] with some color",                line_char = "-")  print_headline("[b]This is a very small[/b] and bold headline",                line_char = ".",                max_width = 60)  # Messages with time stamps test_func <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_step("GREY", "Probably not so important")     print_step("MAJOR", "This is a major step...")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step1")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step2")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step3")     print_step("MAJOR", "[b]Finishing... [/b][#00FFFF with some color again!]")     print_closing() }  test_func()  # See what is going on in the message stack message_stack <- get_message_stack()  # Print messages on the same line instead of below each other in_place_steps <- function(){     print_start_message()          print_step("MAJOR", "Let's get started...")          for (i in seq_len(10)){         print_step("Minor", "This is in place step [i] of 10", i = i, in_place = TRUE)         Sys.sleep(0.25)     }          print_step("MAJOR", "Loop has ended")          print_closing() }  in_place_steps()  # Set up a custom message set_up_custom_message(type           = "HOTDOG",                       color          = "#B27A01",                       ansi_icon      = "\U0001f32d",                       text_icon      = "IOI",                       indent         = 1,                       ansi_wait_icon = "\U00023f1",                       text_wait_icon = "/",)  hotdog_print <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_message("HOTDOG", c("This is the first hotdog message! Hurray!",                               "And it is also multiline in this version."))     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_closing() }  hotdog_print()  # See new message in the message stack hotdog_stack <- get_message_stack()

title: Custom Formatted Console Messages with Timing Support

title: printify
code: # Official release install.packages("printify")  # Development version devtools::install_github("s3rdia/printify")  install.packages('printify', repos = c('https://s3rdia.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))  pak::pak("s3rdia/printify")

title: printify
text: functions package actually talk user show runtime. message system relies pure base R provide built message types, also custom ones.
code: library(printify)  # Example messages print_message("NOTE", c("Just a quick note that you can also insert e.g.[? a / ]variable",                         "name[?s] like this: [listing].",                         "Depending on the number of variables you can also alter the text."),              listing = c("age", "state", "NUTS3"))               #> [1m[38;5;73m ℹ️ NOTE: [0mJust a quick note that you can also insert e.g. variable #> [1m[38;5;73m ℹ️       [0mnames like this: age, state, NUTS3. #> [1m[38;5;73m ℹ️       [0mDepending on the number of variables you can also alter the text.  print_message("WARNING", "Just a quick [#FF00FF colored warning]!")  #> [1m[38;5;214m ⚠️ WARNING: [0mJust a quick [38;5;201mcolored warning[0m[0m!  print_message("ERROR", "Or a [b]bold[/b], [i]italic[/i] and [u]underlined[/u] error.")  #> [1m[38;5;131m ❌ ERROR: [0mOr a [1mbold[22m, [3mitalic[23m and [4munderlined[24m error.  print_message("NEUTRAL", c("You can also just output [u]plain text[/u] if you like and use",                            "[#FFFF00 [b]all the different[/b] [i]formatting options.[/i]]"))                             #> [1m[38;5;73m [0mYou can also just output [4mplain text[24m if you like and use #> [1m[38;5;73m [0m[38;5;226m[1mall the different[22m [3mformatting options.[23m[0m[0m  # Different headlines print_headline("This is a headline")  #>  #> === This is a headline =========================================================  print_headline("[#00FFFF This is a different headline] with some color",                line_char = "-")                 #>  #> --- [38;5;51mThis is a different headline[0m[0m with some color -------------------------------  print_headline("[b]This is a very small[/b] and bold headline",                line_char = ".",                max_width = 60)                 #>  #> ... [1mThis is a very small[22m and bold headline .................  # Messages with time stamps test_func <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_step("GREY", "Probably not so important")     print_step("MAJOR", "This is a major step...")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step1")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step2")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step3")     print_step("MAJOR", "[b]Finishing... [/b][#00FFFF with some color again!]")     print_closing() }  test_func()  #>  #> === [1m[38;5;73mtest_func[0m[0m[22m started at 22:11:58 ============================================== #>  #> [1m[38;5;59m ☁ [0m[3m[38;5;59mProbably not so important[0m[0m[23m[1m[38;5;59m ☁ [0m[3m[38;5;59mProbably not so important[0m[0m[23m [38;5;59m(26 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;40m ➔ [0mThis is a major step... [38;5;59m(13 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;40m    ✚ [0mSub step1 [38;5;59m(12 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;40m    ✚ [0mSub step2 [38;5;59m(41 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;40m    ✚ [0mSub step3 [38;5;59m(12 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;40m ➔ [0m[1mFinishing... [22m[38;5;51mwith some color again![0m[0m [38;5;59m(12 ms)[0m[0m #>  #> === [1m[38;5;73mtest_func[0m[0m[22m execution time: [38;5;76m117 ms[0m[0m ===========================================  # See what is going on in the message stack message_stack <- get_message_stack()  # Set up a custom message hotdog <- set_up_custom_message(ansi_icon = "\U0001f32d",                                 text_icon = "IOI",                                 indent    = 1,                                 type      = "HOTDOG",                                 color     = "#B27A01")  hotdog_print <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_message(hotdog, c("This is the first hotdog message! Hurray!",                             "And it is also multiline in this version."))     print_step(hotdog, "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     print_step(hotdog, "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     print_step(hotdog, "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     print_closing() }  hotdog_print()  #>  #> === [1m[38;5;73mhotdog_print[0m[0m[22m started at 22:11:58 =========================================== #>  #> [1m[38;5;136m 🌭 HOTDOG: [0mThis is the first hotdog message! Hurray! #> [1m[38;5;136m 🌭[0m[0m[22m         And it is also multiline in this version.  #> [1m[38;5;136m 🌭 [0mOr use as single line message with time stamps. [38;5;59m(34 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;136m 🌭 [0mOr use as single line message with time stamps. [38;5;59m(12 ms)[0m[0m #> [1m[38;5;136m 🌭 [0mOr use as single line message with time stamps. [38;5;59m(12 ms)[0m[0m #>  #> === [1m[38;5;73mhotdog_print[0m[0m[22m execution time: [38;5;76m59 ms[0m[0m =========================================  # See new message in the message stack hotdog_stack <- get_message_stack()

title: Convert Color Codes — hex_ansi
text: hex_to_256(): Generate 256-color 6x6x6 color cube. hex_to_ansi(): Applies hex color font weight text ansi codes.

title: Convert Color Codes — hex_ansi
code: hex_to_256(hex_color)  hex_to_ansi(text, hex_color = NULL, bold = FALSE)

title: Convert Color Codes — hex_ansi
text: hex_color hex color code convert. text text contains hex color codes convert ansi style formatting. bold FALSE default. TRUE inserts ansi code bold printing

title: Convert Color Codes — hex_ansi
text: hex_to_256(): Returns 6x6x6 color cube. hex_to_ansi(): Returns formatted text.

title: Convert Color Codes — hex_ansi
code: color_cube <- hex_to_ansi("#32CD32")  formatted_text <- hex_to_ansi("This is a text to test the coloring",                               hex_color = "#32CD32", bold = TRUE)

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
text: Printing styled messages different occasions. notifications, warnings, errors, function call headlines, progress function closing messages. just neutral one.

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
code: print_message(   type,   text,   ...,   always_print = FALSE,   utf8 = .printify_messages[["format"]][["utf8"]],   no_color = .printify_messages[["no_color"]] )  print_headline(   text,   ...,   line_char = "=",   max_width = getOption("width"),   always_print = FALSE,   utf8 = .printify_messages[["format"]][["utf8"]],   no_color = .printify_messages[["no_color"]] )  print_start_message(   current_time = Sys.time(),   caller_color = "#63C2C9",   always_print = FALSE,   suppress = FALSE,   utf8 = .printify_messages[["format"]][["utf8"]],   no_color = .printify_messages[["no_color"]] )  print_closing(   time_threshold = 2,   start_time = .printify_messages[["start_time"]],   caller_color = "#63C2C9",   always_print = FALSE,   suppress = FALSE,   utf8 = .printify_messages[["format"]][["utf8"]],   no_color = .printify_messages[["no_color"]] )  print_step(   type,   text,   ...,   in_place = FALSE,   always_print = FALSE,   utf8 = .printify_messages[["format"]][["utf8"]],   no_color = .printify_messages[["no_color"]] )  set_up_custom_message(   type = "UNICORN",   color = "#FF00FF",   ansi_icon = NULL,   text_icon = "^",   ansi_wait_icon = NULL,   text_wait_icon = "?",   indent = 1,   text_bold = FALSE,   text_italic = FALSE,   text_underline = FALSE,   text_color = NULL,   time_color = "#6B6B6B" )

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
text: type displayed normal note, text displayed front message. also appears type message stack. text message text display. ... Additional information display like variable names. use write [NAME PUT ] text. always_print FALSE default. TRUE, prints headlines even deeper nested situations. utf8 Whether display complex characters just plain text. no_color Whether display color codes suppress . line_char character forms line. max_width maximum number characters drawn, determines line length headline. current_time current time create time stamp. caller_color Hex color code displayed caller function. suppress FALSE default. TRUE triggers message procedures create message stack print message console. time_threshold total time spent displayed different colors green yellow red, depending threshold specified (seconds). start_time time function call started calculate time difference output total time spent. in_place Prints step message line , instead next line. can e.g. used inside loops. color color message type. ansi_icon icon used message displayed utf8 mode. text_icon icon used message displayed text mode. ansi_wait_icon icon used timed message waiting end execution displayed utf8 mode. text_wait_icon icon used timed message waiting end execution displayed text mode. indent many spaces indent message. text_bold FALSE default. TRUE prints message text bold letters. text_italic FALSE default. TRUE prints message text italic letters. text_underline FALSE default. TRUE prints message text underlined. text_color color actual message text. time_color color used time stamps.

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
text: Return text without styling total running time. set_up_custom_message(): Returns global list custom messages.

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
text: message types can enter custom texts, capable using different styling operators. : Insert list elements: [vector_name] Adding conditional words, list elements one element: [?word] Adding conditional singular/plural, depending list element length: [?singular/plural] Bold, italic underline: [b]text[/b], []text[/], [u]text[/u] Coloring parts message: [#FF00FF text]

title: Print Styled Messages — messages
code: # Example messages print_message("NOTE", c("Just a quick note that you can also insert e.g.[? a / ]variable",                         "name[?s] like this: [listing].",                         "Depending on the number of variables you can also alter the text."),              listing = c("age", "state", "NUTS3"))  print_message("WARNING", "Just a quick [#FF00FF colored warning]!")  print_message("ERROR", "Or a [b]bold[/b], [i]italic[/i] and [u]underlined[/u] error.")  print_message("NEUTRAL", c("You can also just output [u]plain text[/u] if you like and use",                            "[#FFFF00 [b]all the different[/b] [i]formatting options.[/i]]"))  # Different headlines print_headline("This is a headline")  print_headline("[#00FFFF This is a different headline] with some color",                line_char = "-")  print_headline("[b]This is a very small[/b] and bold headline",                line_char = ".",                max_width = 60)  # Messages with time stamps test_func <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_step("GREY", "Probably not so important")     print_step("MAJOR", "This is a major step...")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step1")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step2")     print_step("MINOR", "Sub step3")     print_step("MAJOR", "[b]Finishing... [/b][#00FFFF with some color again!]")     print_closing() }  test_func()  # See what is going on in the message stack message_stack <- get_message_stack()  # Print messages on the same line instead of below each other in_place_steps <- function(){     print_start_message()      print_step("MAJOR", "Let's get started...")      for (i in seq_len(10)){         print_step("Minor", "This is in place step [i] of 10", i = i, in_place = TRUE)         Sys.sleep(0.25)     }      print_step("MAJOR", "Loop has ended")      print_closing() }  in_place_steps()  # Set up a custom message set_up_custom_message(type           = "HOTDOG",                       color          = "#B27A01",                       ansi_icon      = "\U0001f32d",                       text_icon      = "IOI",                       ansi_wait_icon = "\U00023f1",                       text_wait_icon = "/",                       indent         = 1)  hotdog_print <- function(){     print_start_message()     print_message("HOTDOG", c("This is the first hotdog message! Hurray!",                               "And it is also multiline in this version."))     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_step("HOTDOG", "Or use as single line message with time stamps.")     Sys.sleep(0.5)     print_closing() }  hotdog_print()  # See new message in the message stack hotdog_stack <- get_message_stack()

title: Message Helper Functions — message_helpers
text: get_message_stack(): Retrieves current message stack list data frame. set_no_print(): FALSE default. set TRUE messages formatted returned printed console. Can e.g. used unit test situations. set_no_color(): TRUE default. Suppresses color codes messages can printed clean. print_stack_as_messages(): Prints message stack actual messages (suppressed messages). Can used trigger expect_message, expect_warning expect_error unit tests. convert_square_brackets(): Convert different curly bracket patterns ansi formatting passes context vectors text.

title: Message Helper Functions — message_helpers
code: get_message_stack(as_data_frame = FALSE)  set_no_print(value = FALSE)  set_no_color(value = TRUE)  print_stack_as_messages(type = NULL)  convert_square_brackets(text, ...)

title: Message Helper Functions — message_helpers
text: as_data_frame FALSE default. TRUE returns message stack information data frame. value Can TRUE FALSE. type message type filter. text text replace curly brackets. ... actual replacement vectors.

title: Message Helper Functions — message_helpers
text: get_message_stack(): Returns list messages data frame. set_no_print(): Returns global no_print option. set_no_color(): Returns global no_color option. print_stack_as_messages(): Returns NULL. convert_square_brackets(): Returns formatted text.

title: Message Helper Functions — message_helpers
text: message types can enter custom texts, capable using different styling operators. : Insert list elements: [vector_name] Adding conditional words, list elements one element: [?word] Adding conditional singular/plural, depending list element length: [?singular/plural] Bold, italic underline: [b]text[/b], []text[/], [u]text[/u] Coloring parts message: [#FF00FF text]

title: printify — printify-package
text: lightweight message system relying purely base R. Comes built-pre styled message types provides easy way create custom messages. Supports individually styled colored text well timing information. Designed make console output informative visually organized.

title: printify — printify-package
text: 4.1.0 higher

title: printify — printify-package
text: #' Messages: print_message(), print_headline(), print_start_message(), print_closing(), print_step(), set_up_custom_message(), get_message_stack(), set_no_print(), set_no_color(), print_stack_as_messages(), convert_square_brackets()

title: printify — printify-package
text: Tim Siebenmorgen

title: printify 1.0.1 - DEVELOPMENT
text: set_no_color(): Suppresses color codes messages can printed clean. option auto controlled load via system variable NO_COLOR can also set individually function. Console output e.g. RStudio vs. output logging system handled automatically rightnow. (25.04.2026, thanks @TroyHernandez) set_up_custom_message(): Waiting symbols well color time stamps can now customized. (25.04.2026) print_step(): Now new in_place parameter, prints message line , instead next line. can e.g. used inside loops follows. (29.04.2026)
code: new_in_place_steps <- function(){     print_start_message()      print_step("MAJOR", "Let's get started...")      for (i in seq_len(10)){         print_step("Minor", "This is in place step [i] of 10", i = i, in_place = TRUE)         Sys.sleep(0.25)     }      print_step("MAJOR", "Loop has ended")      print_closing() }   new_in_place_steps()

title: printify 1.0.1 - DEVELOPMENT
text: set_up_custom_message(): way custom messages set slightly changed break existing code. Custom message types now stored globally called within quotation marks like built-message types. See updated README new handling. (25.04.2026)

title: printify 1.0.1 - DEVELOPMENT
text: print_step(): Waiting character “?” now drawn intended non-utf8 mode. (25.04.2026)

title: printify 1.0.1 - DEVELOPMENT
text: Message time stamps now drawn lighter grey enhance visibility darker themes. (20.04.2026)

title: printify 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN submission.
